question:
Select the option that is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number.

(5, 9, 52)

(11, 7, 90)
Select the odd one out from the following.

In this question, a set of letters/symbols is coded using the numbers according to the table given below and the conditions that follow. Select the correct coded combination for the given word GRAVITY.

Number/Symbol

Y

T

V

R

I

G

E

A

Code

4

6

8

0

3

5

7

9

Conditions:

(i) If the first element is a vowel and the last element is a consonant, then the codes of these two (first and last elements) are interchanged.
(ii) If the first element is a consonant and the last element is a vowel, then the codes of the first and last elements are coded as 2.
(iii) If the third and fifth elements are both vowels, then the fifth element is coded as the code of the third element and vice versa.

What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following number series?

178, 142, 117, 101, 92, ?

Find the value of the question mark (?) based on the pattern followed in the given number groups.

12, 4, 37, 25 = 4

2, 36, 87, 51 = 2

18, 3, 58, 49 = ?

Statements:

Some pencils are pens.

No pen is a sharpener.

All sharpeners are staplers.

Conclusions:

I. At least some staplers are pens.

II. All pencils are pens.

Select the correct conclusion(s) based on the given statements.
option_1: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
option_2: Only conclusion II follows
option_3: Both conclusions I and II follow
option_4: Only conclusion I follows

question:
Directions: Assuming the given statement to be true, identify the assumptions that are necessary to make the statement valid and choose the correct option.

Statement: The rapid development of Artificial Intelligence in the healthcare sector promises improved diagnostic accuracy, but concerns about data privacy hinder its full utilization.

Assumptions:

I. Artificial Intelligence can increase the accuracy of diagnosis in the healthcare sector.

II. Issues related to data privacy are a major challenge in the use of AI in the healthcare sector.

III. There are no concerns related to the use of patient data in the healthcare sector.
option_1: Only I and II are implicit
option_2: Only II and III are implicit
option_3: Only I is implicit
option_4: I, II and III are implicit

question: Guava is the only brother of Grape's father. Mango is the mother of Apple. Apple is the brother of Kiwi. The wife of Apple's maternal grandfather is also the paternal grandmother of Grape. How is Mango related to Guava?

In a class of 98 students, every student plays at least one of the three games – Snooker, Chess and Tennis. 42 students play Snooker, 49 play Tennis and 43 play Chess. The total number of students who play exactly two games is 29. 5 students play all the three games. The number of students who play only Snooker and only Chess is equal. 11 students play only Snooker and Tennis. 6 students play only Snooker and Chess. How many students play only Tennis?
Using the given sheet, identify which of the following cubes cannot be formed.

Some persons are sitting in a row facing north. M sits at the left end. K sits exactly to the right of M and is adjacent to M. G sits exactly to the right of K and is adjacent to K. There are only five persons sitting between G and D. D sits at the sixth position from the right end. G sits somewhere to the left of D. If no other person is sitting in the row, then what is the total number of persons sitting in the row?

B is standing at a point facing north. B turns and runs 50 m towards the east, then turns towards the south and walks 30 m. Then he turns left and runs 40 m. After that he turns right and runs 50 m. He again turns right and walks 10 m, and finally turns right and walks 20 m. How much distance did B cover by walking only and in which direction is he now facing? (All turns are of 90°)

Which of the following interchanges of signs and numbers will make the given equation correct?

6 × 4 + 2 = 16
option_2: Interchanging × with + and 4 with 6
option_3: Interchanging × with + and 4 with 2
option_4: Interchanging × with + and 2 with 6

question: Five years ago, the ratio of the ages of Tarun and Saurabh was 4 : 1. After five years from now, the ratio of their ages will be 2 : 1. What is the present age (in years) of Saurabh?
If it will be Tuesday after exactly 1 year and 2 days from today, then what day is today? (It is not a leap year.)
Identify the figure from the options which, when placed at the position of the question mark (?), will complete the series logically.
How many triangles are there in the given figure?
Select the option that represents the figure obtained when the transparent paper (question figure) is folded along the dotted line.
Select the option figure that is embedded in the given figure (rotation is NOT allowed).
Select the option that is suitable for the blank space in the given figure matrix.
Select the correct water image of the given figure from the four options.

Some persons are standing in a straight line facing north. X stands immediately to the left of W but immediately to the right of R. Z stands immediately to the left of R. There is only one person to the left of P. Q stands immediately to the left of Y. The person standing at the right end is the only person standing to the right of Y. There are only four persons standing between P and Q. If no other person is standing in the row, then what is the total number of persons standing in the row?

Statements: $ < # = & ≤ @ > * = !

Conclusions:

I. * > &

II. # < !

Select the correct conclusion(s) based on the given statements.
option_1: Only conclusion II is true
option_2: Only conclusion I is true
option_3: Both conclusions I and II are true
option_4: Neither conclusion I nor II is true

question:
Select the word from the given options that cannot be formed using the letters of the given word.

CONCENTRATION

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option.

Statement: Although recent advancements in quantum computing have significantly improved processing speeds, the stability of quantum states remains a critical barrier to developing large-scale quantum computers for practical applications.

Assumptions:

I. Quantum computing holds the potential for faster data processing than classical computing.
II. Quantum states are inherently unstable, making large-scale quantum computing unfeasible.
III. Recent advancements in quantum computing have resolved the issue of quantum state stability.
option_1: Only I and II are implicit
option_2: Only II and III are implicit
option_3: Only I is implicit
option_4: All I, II and III are implicit

question: Which one among the following Mahajanapadas in ancient India was an oligarchy?
solution_text: Vajji was a confederacy of clans such as the Licchavis and followed the gana-sangha system, which is considered an oligarchic or republican form of governance. Kosala, Gandhara, and Magadha were monarchies ruled by kings. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question: Which one of the following statements about the Bhoodan Movement is correct?
option_1: The target was to get one-sixth of cultivable land in India as donation
option_2: It was approved through a Central Government Act
option_3: It was ensured that the donated land was free from all litigation
option_4: The first donation of land was in Bihar
solution_text: The Bhoodan Movement was launched by Vinoba Bhave in 1951 to encourage landowners to voluntarily donate land to landless farmers. Its objective was to obtain about one-sixth of India’s cultivable land for redistribution among the poor. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question:
With reference to the strategic and operational framework of the Vibrant Villages Programme-II (VVP-II), consider the following statements:

1. It operates as a Central Sector Scheme featuring complete financial backing from the Union Government to accelerate the multi-sectoral growth of strategic border assets.

2. The institutional mechanism relies on state public works departments to draft localized Village Action Plans.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution_text: Vibrant Villages Programme-II operates as a Central Sector Scheme with full financial support from the Union Government. However, Village Action Plans are prepared mainly by District Administrations in coordination with Gram Panchayats and local communities, not by state public works departments. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question: Which one of the following statements about the Parliament of India is correct?
option_1: A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote
option_2: The Deputy Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha is nominated by the President from among the members of the Rajya Sabha
option_3: The Lok Sabha cannot be dissolved before the completion of its five year term
option_4: The members of Lok Sabha also vote in the election of the members of Rajya Sabha
solution_text: Most members of the Rajya Sabha are elected by the elected members of the State Legislative Assemblies through proportional representation using the single transferable vote system. The Deputy Chairperson is elected by the Rajya Sabha members, the Lok Sabha can be dissolved before five years, and Lok Sabha members do not vote in Rajya Sabha elections. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question:
With reference to the constitutional transformations enacted by the Charter Act of 1833, consider the following statements:

1. It established a singular legislative authority for British India by stripping the Governors of Bombay and Madras of their independent law-making powers.

2. The Act introduced a fourth member into the Governor-General’s Council as a Law Member, who was granted a vote on executive, fiscal, and military decisions.

3. It directed the Governor-General in Council to take active steps toward the mitigation and eventual abolition of slavery within Company territories.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution_text: The Charter Act of 1833 centralized legislative powers by removing the law-making authority of Bombay and Madras Presidencies. It also directed steps toward reducing and abolishing slavery. However, the Law Member in the Governor-General’s Council could participate only in legislative matters and had no voting rights in executive, military, or financial decisions. Therefore, option (b) is correct.

question:
With reference to different chemical fuels, consider the following statements:

1. Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) consists of a mixture of hydrocarbons, with butane and propane being the primary components.

2. Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) is composed of methane gas, which produces fewer polluting emissions compared to petroleum-based fuels.

3. Coal gas is an industrial fuel obtained during the processing of coal to get coke, and its main combustible constituents are methane and carbon monoxide.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution_text: LPG mainly contains propane and butane. CNG is primarily methane and burns cleaner than petroleum fuels. Coal gas is produced during the processing of coal for coke and contains combustible gases such as methane and carbon monoxide. Therefore, all the statements are correct and option (d) is the correct answer.

question: Which one of the following statements regarding oxidation and reduction reactions is NOT correct?
option_1: If a substance loses hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
option_2: If a substance loses oxygen during reaction, it is reduced
option_3: If a substance gains hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
option_4: If a substance gains oxygen during reaction, it is oxidised
solution_text: In oxidation-reduction reactions, oxidation involves gain of oxygen or loss of hydrogen, while reduction involves loss of oxygen or gain of hydrogen. Therefore, a substance losing hydrogen undergoes oxidation, not reduction. Hence, option (a) is incorrect.

question:
Consider the following lines of Longitude and Latitude:

  1. Prime Meridian

  2. Tropic of Cancer

  3. Equator

Which of the above lines is/are a Great Circle?
solution_text: A great circle is formed by a plane passing through the center of the Earth. The Equator is a great circle, and longitudes such as the Prime Meridian form great circles when combined with their opposite meridians. The Tropic of Cancer is not a great circle because it does not pass through the Earth’s center. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question: The lactic acid that gets accumulated in the muscle cells during rigorous exercise causing muscle cramps is produced from which of the following?
solution_text: During intense exercise, muscle cells undergo anaerobic respiration due to limited oxygen supply. In this process, pyruvate produced during glycolysis is converted into lactic acid, which may cause muscle cramps and fatigue. Therefore, option (b) is correct.

question: The scheme PRASHAD (Pilgrimage Rejuvenation & Spiritual Heritage Augmentation Drive) provides assistance for which of the following?
option_3: The development of tourism infrastructures in States & Union Territories
solution_text: The PRASHAD scheme was launched to promote the integrated development of pilgrimage and heritage destinations in India. It focuses on improving tourism infrastructure, amenities, and connectivity at religious and spiritual sites. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question:
With reference to wave propagation, consider the following statements:

1. Sound waves are longitudinal mechanical waves that require a material medium to propagate and cannot travel through a vacuum.

2. Light waves are transverse electromagnetic waves that travel faster in denser optical mediums like glass than in a vacuum.

3. The velocity of sound in air increases with an increase in ambient temperature and humidity.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution_text: Sound waves are mechanical longitudinal waves and require a material medium, so they cannot travel in vacuum. Light waves are electromagnetic waves and travel fastest in vacuum, while their speed decreases in denser mediums like glass. The speed of sound in air increases with higher temperature and humidity. Therefore, statements 1 and 3 are correct and option (c) is the correct answer.

question: The goods whose demand move in the opposite direction of income of consumer are called:
solution_text: Inferior goods are those goods whose demand decreases when consumer income increases and increases when income falls. Consumers usually shift to better-quality alternatives as their income rises. Therefore, option (d) is correct.

question: In the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of India, the largest share in the current years has come from:
solution_text: In recent years, the service sector has contributed the largest share to India’s GDP. It includes areas such as information technology, banking, telecommunications, tourism, healthcare, and education, making it the dominant sector of the Indian economy. Therefore, option (d) is correct.

question:
Which of the following is/are the characteristics of an economy facing a liquidity trap?

  1. High-interest rates

  2. Low savings rate

  3. Low consumer spending
solution_text: A liquidity trap occurs when interest rates are very low and people prefer to hold cash instead of spending or investing it. This results in low consumer spending and weak economic growth. High-interest rates and low savings rates are not typical characteristics of a liquidity trap. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question: What is the legal nature of the Right to Property under Article 300A?
option_2: The State can take property without any legal basis
option_3: A constitutional legal right allowing deprivation only by law
option_4: Enforceable by Article 32 before the Supreme Court
solution_text: Article 300A states that no person can be deprived of property except by authority of law. After the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978, the Right to Property ceased to be a Fundamental Right and became a constitutional legal right. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question: Cheraw, known for dancers stepping between moving bamboo poles, is a folk dance of which state?
solution_text: Cheraw, also known as the Bamboo Dance, is a traditional folk dance of Mizoram. In this dance, performers step skillfully between moving bamboo poles. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question: Which classical economist popularised the phrase "From each according to his ability, to each according to his needs"?
solution_text: The phrase “From each according to his ability, to each according to his needs” was popularised by Karl Marx in his work Critique of the Gotha Programme (1875). It reflects the principle of distribution in a communist society. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question: In which of the following years did India NOT host the ICC Cricket World Cup Tournament?
solution_text: India did not host the ICC Cricket World Cup in 1983. The tournament was hosted by England, where India won its first World Cup title by defeating the West Indies in the final. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question: Who among the following classical poets is the author of the book Kiratarjuniyam?
solution_text: Kiratarjuniyam is a famous Sanskrit Mahakavya written by the poet Bharavi. It describes the encounter between Arjuna and Lord Shiva in the form of a hunter (Kirata). Therefore, option (b) is correct.

question: From amongst the following, identify the correct option that captures the changes made in Article 74(1) of the Constitution of India through the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.
option_1: Executive powers of the Union shall be vested with the President of India
option_2: The Prime Minister has the duty to brief the President about decisions of the Council of Ministers
option_3: The President shall exercise his/her functions on the advice of the Council of Ministers, headed by the Prime Minister
option_4: The President shall have the right to summon or prorogue the Houses of the Parliament
solution_text: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 made it compulsory for the President to act according to the advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister under Article 74(1). This strengthened the parliamentary system and reduced the discretionary powers of the President. Therefore, option (c) is correct.

question:
Which of the following are included in Bharatmala Pariyojana?

  1. Road connectivity to border areas

  2. Development of coastal roads

  3. Connectivity for non-major ports

  4. Integration with Sagarmala

Select the answer using the code given below.
solution_text: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a comprehensive road development programme that includes border road connectivity, coastal and port connectivity roads, development of roads for non-major ports, and integration with Sagarmala for improving transport infrastructure. Therefore, all the given statements are correct and option (d) is the correct answer.

question: Which one among the following is not a greenhouse gas?
solution_text: Greenhouse gases trap heat in the Earth’s atmosphere. Ozone, nitrous oxide, and fluorinated gases are greenhouse gases, whereas sulphur dioxide is mainly an air pollutant and is not considered a greenhouse gas. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's current space missions:

1. The uncrewed Gaganyaan-1 orbital mission deploys a female-looking humanoid robot named “Vyommitra” to monitor cabin pressure, oxygen levels, and life-support modules before human flight.

2. The Aditya-L1 spacecraft is positioned in a halo orbit around the Lagrangian Point 1 (L1) to ensure continuous, unobstructed observation of the Sun without any occultation or eclipses.

3. Lagrangian Point 1 (L1) lies directly behind the Earth in the Earth-Sun line, providing an ideal shadow shield from solar radiation.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution_text: Vyommitra is a humanoid robot designed for the uncrewed Gaganyaan mission to monitor crew module conditions before human spaceflight. Aditya-L1 is placed in a halo orbit around the L1 point for uninterrupted observation of the Sun. However, L1 lies between the Earth and the Sun, while L2 is located behind the Earth. Therefore, statements 1 and 2 are correct and option (a) is the correct answer.

question:
India’s Henley Passport Index ranking released in 2026. Consider the following statements:

  1. The index measures visa-free travel access.

  2. India’s rank among countries increased.

  3. The index is published by the World Bank.

Which are correct?
solution_text: The Henley Passport Index measures the number of destinations that passport holders can visit without a prior visa. India’s ranking improved in the 2026 index. However, the index is published by Henley & Partners, not by the World Bank. Therefore, statements 1 and 2 are correct and option (b) is the correct answer.

question:
With reference to the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023, consider the following statements:

  1. The offence of “sedition” under Section 124A of IPC has been completely removed without any substitute provision.

  2. The new law introduces stricter provisions for offences related to mob lynching.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution_text: In the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023, the term “sedition” has been removed, but a new provision related to acts endangering the sovereignty, unity, and integrity of India has been introduced. Hence, statement 1 is incorrect. The BNS also provides stricter punishments for offences such as mob lynching, making statement 2 correct. Therefore, option (b) is correct.

question: Three red lights on the same road start blinking at the same time and blink repeatedly after every 4, 6, and 8 minutes, respectively. How many times will they blink together in an interval of 3 hours, excluding blinking together at the starting moment?
The sum of five consecutive even numbers is 2720. What is the sum of the third and fifth numbers?
What is the LCM of 0.15, 0.18 and 0.45?

The tree is at a distance of 7.5 m from Subhas and 45 m from the building. The eyes of Subhas, the top of the tree, and the top of the building lie on the same straight line. Find the height (in m) from the ground at which Subhas' eyes are situated.

A prism has an equilateral triangular base with side length 6 cm. Its height starts at 1 cm and increases by 1 cm for each subsequent layer, forming 5 layers in total. What is the total volume of the prism? (Use = 1.732)

question: In a zoo, there are horses and pigeons. If heads are counted, there are 200 heads and if legs are counted, there are 580 legs. How many pigeons are there?
A, B and C started a business. A's capital is 7 times the capital of C and B's capital is 2 times the capital of A. If the total profit is ₹7,920, find the share of C (in ₹).
A 45-litre mixture of petrol and kerosene is in the ratio 5 : 4. Some of it is removed and replaced with pure petrol. The final ratio becomes 7 : 4. How much of the mixture was replaced?

Two workers, Sagar and Manish, are working on a heavy machine to melt waste metal. Sagar can complete the work in 15 days, while both workers working on alternate days can complete the work in 12 days. In how many days can Manish alone complete the work?

A bullet train covers a fixed distance in 30 minutes at an average speed of 360 km/h. Due to track maintenance, it needs to be diverted, increasing the distance by 25%. If the train needs to arrive at its destination on time (i.e., in 30 minutes), what should its new average speed be in km/h?

Two friends P and Q simultaneously start running from the same point around a circular track in the same direction. P runs at 6 m/sec and Q runs at b m/sec. If they cross each other at exactly two points on the circular track and b is a natural number less than 6, how many possible values can b take?

The price of cooking oil increased by 9%. A family uses 15 litres of cooking oil per month. If the original price was ₹170 per litre, what is the additional annual expenditure (to the nearest rupee) for the family due to the price hike?
A bookseller sells 12 pens for ₹240, but in doing so, he incurs a loss equal to the cost price of 6 pens. What is the loss percent?

A bank charges simple interest for ₹K at the rate of for n² years. Another bank charges simple interest for ₹L at the rate of for n³ years and gives the same interest. What is the ratio between K and L?
option_1: :
option_2: :

question: In a group of 30 boys, the average height was initially determined to be 150 cm. However, it was later discovered that the height of one boy was incorrectly recorded as 154 cm when it should have been 145 cm. Calculate the correct average height.

The following table shows the units sold of three products (A, B and C) in the months of January, February and March:

Which product had the lowest average units sold?
option_1: B & C both (B और C दोनों)

question: In triangle ABC, if the orthocenter is (9, −3) and the circumcenter is (0, 6), find the coordinates of the centroid.
What is the value of cos⁻¹ − cos⁻¹?
option_1: cos⁻¹
option_2: cot⁻¹
option_3: tan⁻¹
option_4: sin⁻¹

question: If p = , then the value of   is:

option_1: 98()
option_2: 98()
option_3: 98()
option_4: 98()

question: In a trapezoid PQRS with PQ ∥ SR, the diagonals PR and QS intersect at T. What is the ratio of the area of △PQT to the area of △SRT?
option_1: The ratio of PQ to SR squared.
option_2: The ratio of PQ to SR.
option_3: The ratio of the perimeter of △PQT to the perimeter of △SRT.
option_4: The ratio of the area of △PQT to the area of △SRT.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the following sentence.

I just went to Bali on a backpacking trip and did everything by the ear without any itinerary.
option_1: By spreading rumors
solution_text: The idiom "by the ear" means doing something without a fixed plan. It means relying on instinct or improvisation. Option 2 matches this meaning. Option 1 is wrong because spreading rumors means sharing false information. Option 3 is wrong because criticising means finding faults. Option 4 is wrong because asking means requesting information.

question: Which of the following words is the synonym of "pejorative"?
solution_text: "Pejorative" means expressing contempt, disapproval, or something derogatory. "Disparaging" has a similar meaning and refers to something insulting or belittling. Hence, both words are synonymous. "Fickle" means changeable, "Promulgated" means officially announced or spread, and "Diaphanous" means light and translucent.

question:
Change the following direct sentence into indirect speech.

The cybersecurity director declared, "The system credentials must have leaked yesterday, so we are tracking the lateral movement now."
option_1: The cybersecurity director declared that the system credentials had to leak the day before, so they were tracking the lateral movement then.
option_2: The cybersecurity director declared that the system credentials must have leaked the day before, so they were tracking the lateral movement then.
option_3: The cybersecurity director declared that the system credentials must have leaked yesterday, so we were tracking the lateral movement at that time.
option_4: The cybersecurity director declared that the system credentials had leaked the previous day, so they were tracking the lateral movement then.
solution_text: The phrase "must have leaked" expresses a logical deduction about the past, so it remains unchanged in indirect speech. "Yesterday" changes to "the day before," "we" changes to "they," "are tracking" changes to "were tracking," and "now" changes to "then." Therefore, option (b) is grammatically correct.

question:
Directions: In this section, each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and the sixth sentences are given as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences have been jumbled and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences.

S1: An economic crisis is a situation in which the economy of a country experiences a sudden downturn brought on by a financial crisis.

S6: Additionally, central banks also have an important role in reducing the probability of a crisis occurring by undertaking pre-emptive measures that reduce systemic risks.

P: A central bank’s main objective during a crisis is to contain the damage and limit the impact of the crisis on the real economy.

Q: Economic crises not only affect economic activity but can also cause financial panic, reducing policy efficiency.

R: An economic crisis can take the form of a recession or a depression.

S: This can be achieved by enhancing confidence, calming markets, and ensuring smooth credit flow.
solution_text: Sentence R explains the forms of economic crisis after the introduction. Sentence Q explains the effects of the crisis. Sentence P introduces the role of the central bank. Sentence S explains how this role is carried out. Therefore, the correct order is RQPS.

question:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word.

We should not run after transient pleasures of the world.
solution_text: The word "transient" means temporary or short-lived. Option 2 is correct because permanent means lasting forever. Option 1 is wrong because sinful relates to moral behavior. Option 3 is wrong because joyful relates to happiness. Option 4 is wrong because unnatural relates to nature.

question:
In the following question, a sentence has been divided into four parts. One of these four parts may or may not be grammatically correct. Choose the part that contains an error and mark it as your answer. If there is no error in any part, mark option (E) as your answer.

The real impact of technology in education lies in how thoughtfully schools integrate AI, (A) / while the priority should be on building AI literacy rather than simply teaching its usage, (B) / ensuring that educators are equipped and trained alongside students, (C) / and reinforce the deeply human connections that sit at the heart of meaningful learning. (D) / No error (E)
option_1: A
option_2: B
option_3: C
option_4: D
solution_text: The error is in part (D). The verb "reinforce" breaks the parallel structure of the sentence. Since part (C) uses the present participle "ensuring," the coordinated structure after "and" should also use the present participle form "reinforcing." Therefore, "reinforce" should be replaced with "reinforcing" to maintain grammatical parallelism.

question:
Change the following active voice sentence into passive voice.

"The restaurant will offer a significant discount on vegetarian dishes throughout the summer season."
option_1: A significant discount on vegetarian dishes will be offered by the restaurant throughout the summer season.
option_2: Throughout the summer season, the restaurant will be offered a significant discount on vegetarian dishes.
option_3: A significant discount will offer the restaurant on vegetarian dishes throughout the summer season.
option_4: Throughout the summer season, a significant discount on vegetarian dishes is to be offered by the restaurant.
solution_text: In passive voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject. Option 1 correctly follows the passive structure using "will be offered by the restaurant". Option 2 changes the meaning. Option 3 has incorrect verb usage. Option 4 changes the tense of the sentence.

question:
Choose the word that can beautifully substitute the given phrase:

"A person who falsely and hypocritically claims to possess special knowledge, magical powers, or medical skills, frequently operating as a fraudulent practitioner to deceive the public for personal or financial gain."
solution_text: "Charlatan" refers to a fraud or impostor who falsely claims to possess special knowledge, skills, or powers in order to deceive people for personal gain. "Anomie" means social instability caused by breakdown of values, "Synecdoche" is a figure of speech, and "Inchoate" means undeveloped or incomplete.

question:
Select the word segment that substitutes the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. Select the option 'No correction required' if the sentence is correct as given.

I know how devoted (to the precious Code and the honour you are, Gabriel).
option_1: To the precious Code and your honour you are, Gabriel
option_2: To the precious Code and ones honour you are, Gabriel
option_3: No correction required
option_4: To the precious Code and an honour you are, Gabriel
solution_text: The sentence talks about Gabriel’s own honour, so the pronoun "your" is needed. Using "the honour" is incorrect here. Option 2 uses an incorrect pronoun. Option 3 keeps the error. Option 4 uses a wrong article.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given word.

Termagant
option_1: One who speaks many languages
option_2: Over scrupulous about minute details
option_3: A magnet with strong polarity
option_4: A violent, overbearing, turbulent, brawling, quarrelsome woman
solution_text: The word "termagant" refers to a woman who is aggressive and quarrelsome. Option 1 means polyglot. Option 2 refers to a pedantic person. Option 3 refers to a type of magnet.

question:
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.

One can never imagine / how hot it gets in Delhi / unless one doesn’t go / there in person.
option_1: One can never imagine
option_2: Unless one doesn’t go
option_3: How hot it gets in Delhi
option_4: There in person.
solution_text: The word "unless" already gives a negative meaning. Using "doesn’t" after "unless" is incorrect. The correct form should be "unless one goes". Therefore, option 2 contains the grammatical error. The other parts are correct.

question:
Choose the option that most accurately describes the meaning of the underlined idiom in the given context:

"The finance minister realized that allocating massive emergency funds to the struggling public sector unit was merely throwing good money after bad, as the company’s structural flaws were too deep to be fixed by capital injection alone."
option_1: To misappropriate public funds for personal political gain.
option_2: To spend more money on a losing investment or project that has already failed.
option_3: To make a highly profitable and calculated speculative investment in a risky market.
option_4: To borrow capital at a very high interest rate to clear an existing debt.
solution_text: The idiom "throwing good money after bad" means continuing to invest money, effort, or resources into something that is already failing and unlikely to succeed. In the given context, the government continues funding a structurally weak organization despite little hope of recovery. Therefore, option (b) is the correct interpretation.

question:
Fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb.

He had promised to come but he never ______.
solution_text: "Turned up" means to arrive at a place. The sentence talks about someone who promised to come but did not arrive. Option 2 means to reverse or replace. Option 3 means the result of an event. Option 4 means to move away.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.

Hit the hay
option_1: To take revenge
option_2: To go to bed
option_3: To buy or sell something
option_4: A sarcastic response
solution_text: The idiom "hit the hay" means to go to bed or sleep. Option 1 is about revenge. Option 3 is about business. Option 4 refers to speech, not sleeping.

question: Find out the correct spelling.
solution_text: "Lieutenant" is the correct spelling of the military rank. The other options have incorrect letters and spelling.

question:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.

Each member of the literary club submitted various literary works for the magazine.
option_1: Various literary works had been submitted by each member of the literary club for the magazine.
option_2: Various literary works are submitted by each member of the literary club for the magazine.
option_3: Various literary works were submitted by each member of the literary club for the magazine.
option_4: Various literary works were being submitted by each member of the literary club for the magazine.
solution_text: The given sentence is in simple past tense. In passive voice, simple past tense uses "was/were" with the past participle. Option 3 correctly changes the object into the subject and keeps the tense same. Option 1 changes the tense. Option 2 is in present tense. Option 4 changes the meaning by using continuous form.

question:
Fill in the blank with a suitable homophone.

The flavors in the dish ______ each other perfectly.
solution_text: The word "complement" means to add to something in a way that improves it. The flavors improve each other, so option 2 is correct. Option 1 means praise. Option 3 means willing to please. Option 4 means overly satisfied.

question:
In the following question, a sentence has been given in indirect speech. Select the option which best expresses the same sentence in direct speech.

He exclaimed with regret that he was perished by the people he had made.
option_1: He said, "Alas! I perished by the people I made."
option_2: He said, "Alas! I have perished by the people I have made."
option_3: He said, "Alas! I am perished by the people I have made."
option_4: He said, "Alas! I am perished by the people I made."
solution_text: The sentence shows regret, so the interjection "Alas!" is used. The pronoun changes from "he" to "I". The tense and structure are correctly matched in option 3. Other options have tense or structure problems.

question:
Directions: Look at the underlined part of the sentence. Choose the best substitution for the underlined part. If no improvement is needed, select option 4.

Hardly had he reached home when the telephone rang.
option_1: He had reached
option_2: Did he reached
solution_text: The structure "Hardly had + subject + past participle + when" is correct. The given sentence already follows this rule. The other options break the correct inversion pattern. Therefore, no improvement is required.

question:
Parts of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A) Thus, in India, the people live chiefly on different kinds of grain, eggs, milk, or sometimes fish and meat.

B) The people of which it is made up eat different kinds of food, partly according to the climate and partly according to local production.

C) In the Arctic regions, where no grain or fruit is produced, people live almost entirely on flesh.

D) The human race is spread all over the world, from the polar regions to the tropics.

E) In Europe, people eat more flesh and less grain.
solution_text: Sentence D introduces the topic. Sentence B explains food habits. Sentence A gives the example of India. Sentence E gives the example of Europe. Sentence C talks about the Arctic region. This order forms a clear and logical paragraph.

question:
Read the passage and answer the question.

Our architecture, our education and our dictionaries tell us that space is three-dimensional. The OED defines it as ‘a continuous area or expanse which is free, available or unoccupied … The dimensions of height, depth and width, within which all things exist and move.’ In the 18th century, Immanuel Kant argued that three-dimensional Euclidean space is an a priori necessity and, saturated as we are now in computer-generated imagery and video games, we are constantly subjected to representations of a seemingly axiomatic Cartesian grid. From the perspective of the 21st century, this seems almost self-evident. Yet the notion that we inhabit a space with any mathematical structure is a radical innovation of Western culture, necessitating an overthrow of long-held beliefs about the nature of reality. Although the birth of modern science is often discussed as a transition to a mechanistic account of nature, arguably more important – and certainly more enduring – is the transformation it entrained in our conception of space as a geometrical construct. Over the past century, the quest to describe the geometry of space has become a major project in theoretical physics, with experts from Albert Einstein onwards attempting to explain all the fundamental forces of nature as byproducts of the shape of space itself. While on the local level we are trained to think of space as having three dimensions, general relativity paints a picture of a four-dimensional universe, and string theory says it has 10 dimensions – or 11 if you take an extended version known as M-Theory. There are variations of the theory in 26 dimensions, and recently pure mathematicians have been electrified by a version describing spaces of 24 dimensions. But what are these ‘dimensions? And what does it mean to talk about a 10-dimensional space of being? In order to come to the modern mathematical mode of thinking about space, one first has to conceive of it as some kind of arena that matter might occupy. At the very least, ‘space’ has to be thought of as something extended. Obvious though this might seem to us, such an idea was anathema to Aristotle, whose concepts about the physical world dominated Western thinking in late antiquity and the Middle Ages. Strictly speaking, Aristotelian physics didn’t include a theory of space, only a concept of place. Think of a cup sitting on a table. For Aristotle, the cup is surrounded by air, itself a substance. In his world picture, there is no such thing as empty space; there are only boundaries between one kind of substance, the cup, and another, the air. Or the table. For Aristotle, ‘space’ (if you want to call it that), was merely the infinitesimally thin boundary between the cup and what surrounds it.

What is the closest synonym for the word “anathema” as used in the passage?
solution_text: The word "anathema" means something that is strongly disliked or hated. Option 3 is correct because "repugnant" means extremely unacceptable or offensive. Option 1 and 2 mean approval, which is opposite. Option 4 means common, which does not fit the meaning.

For Aristotle, ‘space’ (if you want to call it that), was merely the infinitesimally thin boundary between the cup and what surrounds it.

What can be concluded about modern theories of space?
option_1: They universally accept only three dimensions.
option_2: They are based solely on Aristotle’s ideas.
option_3: They reject all mathematical models.
option_4: They have expanded the concept of space to include many dimensions, far beyond the classical three.
solution_text: The passage explains that modern theories describe space as having many dimensions, such as four, ten, or even more. Option 4 correctly reflects this idea. Options 1, 2, and 3 are incorrect because they go against the information given in the passage.

Read the passage and answer the question.

For Aristotle, ‘space’ (if you want to call it that), was merely the infinitesimally thin boundary between the cup and what surrounds it.

According to the passage, how did Aristotle’s concept of space differ from the modern view?
option_1: He believed space was infinite.
option_2: He thought of space not as an empty expanse but as the boundary between substances, without the idea of empty space.
option_3: He believed space had four dimensions.
option_4: He was the first to propose string theory.
solution_text: The passage says Aristotle did not believe in empty space. He thought space was only the boundary between objects and substances. Option 2 correctly explains this view. The other options are not mentioned or are incorrect.

What is the tone of the passage?
option_1: Explanatory and analytical

Read the passage and answer the question.

What is the main idea of the passage?
option_1: The concept of space as a mathematical and multi-dimensional construct is a modern innovation, distinct from earlier ideas like Aristotle’s concept of place.
option_2: Aristotle was wrong about everything in physics.
option_3: Dimensions are only useful in video games.
option_4: The OED’s definition of space is outdated.
solution_text: The passage focuses on how the idea of space has changed over time. It contrasts modern mathematical views with Aristotle’s older concept of place. Option 1 correctly summarizes this main idea. The other options are either extreme or incorrect.
